member.name: M:_3PS.Controllers.ReportingController.PostVaccineReport(_3PS.Models.Reporting.VaccineReporting)
member.summary: Submits a new vaccine report to be saved.
member.remarks: This endpoint receives vaccine report details and persists them. Note: This saves data using the VaccineReporting model, which is configured in the DbContext to map to the "DBA - VaccineBatchSendReport" table using the string 'RECNUM' property as the key.
member.param: The vaccine report data based on the VaccineReporting model.
member.returns: A status code indicating the result of the operation.
member.response: Report successfully created. Returns the RECNUM.
Bad Request. The report data was null or invalid.
Internal Server Error. An error occurred processing the request.

member.name: T:_3PS.Controllers.VaccineController
member.summary: Provides endpoints for accessing vaccine-related data, such as batch send information.

member.name: M:_3PS.Controllers.VaccineController.GetVaccineBatchSendData(System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets records from the vaccine batch send data source with pagination and optional filtering.
member.remarks:
Retrieves data corresponding to the VwVaccineBatchSend model (sourced from DbContext configuration). Supports filtering by the 'SendingBatch' number. Results are ordered by Ticket number.
**Pagination:** - Uses query parameters `pageNumber` and `pageSize`. - Default page size is 10, maximum is 50. Invalid sizes are clamped to the nearest valid value. - Pagination metadata is returned in custom 'X-Pagination-*' response headers. Ensure your client is configured to read these headers (requires 'Access-Control-Expose-Headers').
member.param: Optional: Filter records by the 'SendingBatch' number.
The page number to retrieve (must be 1 or greater, defaults to 1 if invalid).
The number of items per page (must be between 1 and 50, defaults to 10, clamped if invalid).
member.returns: A paginated list of vaccine batch send records.
member.response: Returns the requested page of vaccine batch send records. Pagination details are in the 'X-Pagination-*' headers.
Bad Request. Indicates an issue with the request parameters (though this implementation often defaults/clamps instead of returning 400 for simple range errors).
Internal Server Error. An error occurred while retrieving the data.
